response.justification: James Smith is a versatile full-stack and backend engineer with solid TypeScript/Node.js and distributed systems exposure, but he critically fails to meet the role's non-negotiable minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly 6 months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis side project, with C++ absent entirely from the skills section. Similarly, the 5-year requirement for large-scale infrastructure and compute technologies is only superficially addressed by one high-throughput microservices role at Dunnhumby and some Kubernetes/Docker DevOps work. There is no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, formal technical leadership of infrastructure teams, or production cloud architecture at the depth the role demands.
strengths: Strong engineering discipline — consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and software craftsmanship across multiple roles
Breadth of distributed/high-throughput systems exposure, including low-latency async I/O services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Demonstrated mentoring and knowledge-sharing habits through workshops, code dojos, and conference talks
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months vs. the required 8 years), and the language does not appear in the candidate's own skills list
No evidence of technical leadership over a cloud compute or infrastructure platform, which is the core scope of this role
Cloud architecture depth is shallow — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz and Kubernetes deployments for a side project do not constitute production-level cloud platform ownership
